title: salix phylicifolia subsp. basaltica
intro: Salix phylicifolia subsp. basaltica, also known as the basalt willow, is a subspecies of the willow tree Salix phylicifolia, native to Scotland and Ireland. It is a shrub-like plant with small leaves and catkins, used in traditional herbal medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to reduce pain, fever, and inflammation, and promote wound healing and skin health. In herbal preparations, the bark and leaves of the Salix phylicifolia subsp. basaltica can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
